Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given a scenario and need to construct an accepting Petri net for the insurance claim management process Steps 1 Create a Petri net 2 Create places for the states including a start and end place and intermediate places between activities 3 Create transitions for each activity 4 Add arcs to connect the places and transitions in the correct order including loops for rework

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We note the activities transitions and the states places we might need

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Initial state new claim arrives We have a start place p0 with a token in the initial marking

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1 Claim Registration CR after CR we are in a state ready for Document Verification 2 Document Verification DV if documents are complete we proceed to Fraud Check FC If not we go to Re Document Submission RDS and then back to DV 3 Fraud Check FC if no fraud we go to Claim Assessment CA If fraud is suspected we go to In depth Investigation II and then back to FC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 4 Claim Assessment CA then we go to either Approval AP or directly to Payment P if the amount is below a threshold But the scenario says The claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold So we need to model a choice

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note that the scenario does not specify an activity for when the amount is below the threshold So we can model two transitions after CA one for Approval AP and one for bypassing approval lets call it Auto Approval or just skip to payment But the scenario only lists AP Alternatively we can have a silent transition no label for the bypass or we can model a condition in the net

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since we are to use labeled transitions for activities we can do After CA we have two possible transitions AP if amount above threshold A silent transition if amount below threshold to move to the state before payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note the scenario says The claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold So we can model CA to place after CA then two outgoing arcs one to AP and one to an invisible transition Then both AP and the invisible transition lead to the same place before payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 5 Payment P 6 Notification N 7 Closure CL We also note that after payment we notify and then close So we can have P to place after payment to N to place after notification to CL to place closed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However the scenario says once the claim is paid a notification is sent and finally the claim is closed So the order is P then N then CL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to model the loops For DV and RDS We have a place after CR lets call it after CR that goes to DV After DV if complete we go to a place before FC If not we go to RDS and then back to DV

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually we want to go back to DV so we can have RDS to after CR which is the place before DV But note the token in after CR would then fire DV again

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly for FC and II Place before FC input to FC Transition FC Output places before CA if no fraud suspected if fraud suspected Then from suspected we have transition II which leads to before FC so we can repeat FC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also note that the process starts with a token in the start place and then goes to CR So we have start to CR to after CR And the process ends at a place after CL closed The final marking should have a token in closed

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets list the places we need start after CR incomplete before FC suspected before CA after CA before P after P after N closed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the bypass invisible transition and AP both lead to the same place before P So we can have after CA to AP to before P and after CA to invisible transition to before P

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we can have two separate places But that would be unnecessary We can have a place that has two outgoing arcs to two transitions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Place after CA has two outgoing arcs one to AP and one to invisible transition Then both AP and invisible transition have an arc to before P

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets create the net Steps in code 1 Create the Petri net 2 Create all the places and transitions 3 Add the arcs 4 Set the initial marking token in start 5 Set the final marking token in closed

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We will use the following names for transitions with labels as described CR Claim Registration DV Document Verification RDS Re Document Submission FC Fraud Check II In depth Investigation CA Claim Assessment AP Approval P Payment N Notification CL Closure We also have an invisible transition for the bypass after CA
